---
title: "Research"
subtitle: "Cybersecurity experimentation infrastructure, measurement, and reproducibility"
toc: true
toc-title: "On this page"
---

My research builds the infrastructure, methods, and metrics that make
cybersecurity and networking experimentation **rigorous, reproducible, and
useful to the broader community**. Below are the main threads, along with the
platforms my team has designed and operated.

## Research Threads

### Cybersecurity Experimentation Infrastructure
Developing and maintaining networked cybersecurity experimentation and
data-sharing platforms — from semantic frameworks for analyzing experiment data
to orchestration of complex, multi-domain experiments.

### Measurements, Metrics & Benchmarks
Developing robust measurements and user-centric metrics with the goal of
creating rigorous, community-adopted benchmarks — including accurate measurement
of denial-of-service impact and defense effectiveness.

### Reproducibility & Repeatability
Experimentation methodology for repeatability and reproducibility in networking
research, including replicated testbed studies and fidelity analyses of traffic
generation and network emulation.

### Cyber-Physical Systems & Cross-Disciplinary Modeling
Statistical modeling of complex, interconnected techno-economic-social systems —
for example, security and resiliency of energy cyber-physical systems and
strategic adversaries in dynamic-pricing markets.

### Data Collection for Machine Learning
Leading large-scale, representative data-collection efforts (such as the DARPA
SearchLight application-traffic dataset) to enable machine-learning research on
realistic network behavior.

### Community, Tutorials & Workshops
Cultivating research communities and disseminating best practices through
tutorials, workshops, and shared testbed resources.

## Infrastructure I Build & Operate

### DeterLab
For **19+ years**, the only public research infrastructure dedicated to
cybersecurity (NSF, DARPA, and DHS funded). To date it has served **389 research
projects, 278 institutions, 1,042 researchers** across **205 locations in 46
countries**.

### Merge (MergeTB)
Control software for experimental environments, deployed to run three independent
infrastructures: **DARPA DCOMP**, **DARPA SearchLight**, and **NSF DeterLab**.

### Experimentation Tools
| Tool | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| **LegoTG / Flooder** | Realistic traffic generation |
| **VTC generator** | Representative video-teleconference traffic |
| **Network emulator** | High-fidelity network emulation |
| **FARM (dash)** | Human user-behavior models |
| **MAGI** | Experiment automation |
| **ThirdEye** | Experiment logging & monitoring |
| **DDoS benchmarks** | Benchmarking suite |

## Testing & Evaluation

USC-ISI has extensive experience in **testing & evaluation (T&E)** for DARPA
programs. Evaluations are structured as claims and hypotheses aligned with
solicitation-defined metrics, exercised across a wide range of scenarios to
surface both strengths and limitations, with iterative assessments delivered to
the funding agency. Most recently, my team partnered with **Sandia National
Laboratories** to lead the evaluation of three technologies in the DARPA
**SearchLight** program, delivering an **800+ page** final assessment.

## Secure & Classified Facilities

USC-ISI operates a dedicated IT team and infrastructure supporting
export-controlled and classified work, including a **NIST SP 800-171 enclave**,
**SCIFs**, and **DOD-accredited** secure spaces and IT systems in Los Angeles, CA
and Arlington, VA.
